data.description: The decorative Mandelbrot SVGs in the note container are overlapping/colliding with the text on mobile viewports. This should never happen — padding, z-index, and layout should guarantee the SVGs sit behind/around the text without intersecting it. Scope: (1) locate the note container component (likely src/components/notes/* or a shared Note component); (2) figure out whether the SVGs are absolutely-positioned background decorations or inline siblings; (3) fix the collision via correct padding, container sizing, or repositioning the SVGs so they never overlap text content at any viewport width — but especially 375px mobile baseline; (4) verify across short and long note copy. Acceptance: at all viewport widths from 320px up, the Mandelbrot SVGs and note text do not visually collide or overlap. Mobile-first.
data.title: Mandelbrot SVGs collide with text in note container on mobile

data.body: Scope note: the fix for Mandelbrot/text collision may not be a local Visit-page tweak — it likely belongs in DESIGN.md as a sitewide rule (decorative SVG vs text layering), or as a reusable utility class/component. Plan should evaluate: is this a one-off layout fix, or do we need to codify the pattern for any decorative SVG over text container? If the latter, ship a small design utility + DESIGN.md note alongside the local fix.

Review pass. Verified against plan: (1) NeighborhoodPage Note container padding bumped to p-9 md:px-10 md:py-8 — satisfies size='sm' safe-padding minimum (36px = inset 6 + icon 30). (2) DocumentBadge non-featured branch p-5 -> p-6 — satisfies size='xs' minimum (24px). Featured branch p-6 md:p-8 unchanged. (3) DESIGN.md adds 'Mandelbrot corners on text containers' subsection right after the Button default Mandelbrot motif section (line ~352), with invariant prose plus per-size padding table (xs/sm/md/lg). (4) MandelbrotCorners.tsx JSDoc gains a 'Safe-padding invariant' block with per-size minimums and a pointer to DESIGN.md. Placement is in the existing JSDoc above CornerDecorations (the first export); rule applies to both exports and is discoverable. No collateral edits beyond the 4 expected files. pnpm typecheck passes clean. Visual collision sweep at 320/360/375/414 deferred to human.
